purpose: Defines the `!inspect [dashboardId] {task_id}` command for deep-diving into a specific task. Reads initialization.json (find agent entry), the task's progress file (lifecycle data), the canonical plan (full description/approach/files), filtered logs.json entries, and ALL other progress files (to resolve dependency and downstream statuses). Renders a comprehensive single-task report covering Status, Timeline, Agent, Milestones, Deviations, upstream Dependencies, downstream Blocks, Context, Critical Details, Files, Worker Logs, and Dashboard Logs. Read-only.
gotchas: Must read ALL other progress files (not just the target task's) to resolve dependency statuses and downstream blocks
If no progress file exists for the target task, the task is treated as 'pending' with no lifecycle data — many sections will be empty/skipped
Context, Critical Details, and Files come from plan.json (not initialization.json) — that's where the deep-thinking output lives
Worker Logs come from progress file logs[] array; Dashboard Logs come from logs.json filtered by task_id — these are different sources
patterns: 7-step procedure: parse → read init → read target progress → read plan → read+filter logs → read other progress files → render report

Report sections in fixed order: Status/Wave/Layer/Directory header → Timeline table → Agent → Milestones → Deviations → Dependencies (needs) → Blocks (downstream) → Context → Critical Details → Files → Worker Logs → Dashboard Logs
Cross-task resolution: depends_on entries get status emoji from THEIR progress files, downstream blocks get same treatment
conventions: Status emojis used inline in each section header
Two-column tables (event/timestamp, time/label) for Timeline and Milestones
Bulleted lists for Dependencies and Blocks with cross-referenced statuses
